description: One of China's three major museums, featuring a rich collection and six main exhibition halls including the History Hall, Art Hall, and Republic of China Hall.
A top-tier shopping center in Nanjing, gathering internationally renowned brands, located in the core of the Xinjiekou commercial district.
Qinhuai River water sightseeing experience, showcasing ancient architecture and night views along the banks, capturing the charm of the Six Dynasties capital.
A group of mansions from the Republic of China era, a well-preserved historical district with elegant architectural styles.
A preservation district of Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, blending traditional residences with modern commerce, offering a glimpse of old Nanjing's charm.
A double-helix scenic pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark with views of the river and city skyline.
The mausoleum of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, the founding father of the Republic of China, a core attraction in the Zhongshan Scenic Area.
A famous food street in Nanjing, featuring a variety of local snacks and night market delicacies, offering an authentic taste of Nanjing.

attraction_name: Nanjing City Wall Taicheng Scenic Area
description: The best-preserved section of the Ming Dynasty city wall, where visitors can climb up to enjoy panoramic views of Xuanwu Lake.
A double-deck railway and highway bridge independently designed and constructed in China, serving as a city landmark.
China's first national institution of higher learning, located in the core scenic area of the Qinhuai River, with well-preserved ancient architecture.
The ruins of an ancient Southern Dynasty temple, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic structure.
The world's best-preserved ancient castle-style city gate, an important part of the Nanjing Ming City Wall.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory of the Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: The birthplace of modern Chinese astronomy, offering panoramic views of Nanjing and serving as a science education base.
A sacred site commemorating the New Democratic Revolution in China, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
